# Orbi

Orbi is an AI-agent-driven flight booking platform. Users search for
flights using plain natural-language queries (e.g. "London to New York
next Friday" or "cheap flights from London this weekend, anywhere"), and
Orbi parses the request and returns real flight offers.

## How it works

- Natural-language flight search is powered by an LLM (Z.AI's
  GLM-4-32B model) that parses free-text queries into structured search
  parameters.
- Flight data and booking are sourced from Duffel, a flight-booking API
  aggregating 300+ airlines. Orbi's current deployment runs against
  Duffel's sandbox environment (test data, not live production airline
  inventory).
- Payment is processed via Stripe. Orbi's current deployment runs Stripe
  in test mode (no real charges).
- Users can save passenger details once (name, date of birth, passport
  info) for faster repeat booking.
- Orbi supports round-trip, multi-city, and flexible-date search, with a
  price calendar and price-drop tracking for saved searches.

## What Orbi is not

Orbi is a search-and-booking product, not a travel agency, airline, or
ticket reseller. It does not currently connect to live production airline
inventory or process real payments in its current deployment - this file
describes the sandbox/test-mode deployment accurately rather than
overstating production readiness.

## Contact

Support: https://orbi.travel/support
